description: Schema for Dagu DAG YAML format. Dagu uses YAML files to define Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) for workflow orchestration.

name.description: Name of the DAG. If omitted, defaults to the YAML filename without extension.

description.description: A brief description explaining what this DAG does. This helps document the DAG's purpose.

group.description: An organizational label used to group related DAGs together. Useful for categorizing DAGs in the UI, e.g., 'DailyJobs', 'Analytics'.

oneOf.description: Path to a .env file to load environment variables from
List of paths to .env files to load environment variables from. Files can be specified as absolute paths, or relative to: DAG file directory, base config directory, or user's home directory.

dotenv.description: Specifies .env files to load environment variables from. By default, '.env' is loaded. When files are specified, '.env' is automatically prepended to the list. All files are loaded sequentially with later files overriding earlier values. Set to empty array [] to disable all .env loading. Files are loaded relative to the DAG's working_dir.

working_dir.description: Working directory for the DAG. All relative paths (including dotenv files) are resolved relative to this directory. Defaults to the directory containing the DAG file.

oneOf.description: Single cron expression for starting the DAG (e.g., '5 4 * * *' runs daily at 04:05)

expression.description: Cron expression for a recurring schedule entry

oneOf.description: Typed cron schedule entry

at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp with explicit offset and minute precision for a one-off schedule entry

oneOf.description: Typed one-off schedule entry
Multiple schedule entries for starting the DAG at different times
Single cron expression for starting the DAG
Single typed cron schedule entry for starting the DAG
Single typed one-off schedule entry for starting the DAG
Multiple schedule entries for starting the DAG
Single cron expression for stopping the DAG
Single typed cron schedule entry for stopping the DAG
Multiple cron expressions for stopping the DAG
Single cron expression for restarting the DAG
Single typed cron schedule entry for restarting the DAG
Multiple cron expressions for restarting the DAG
Advanced scheduling with separate start, stop, and restart schedules

schedule.description: Schedule configuration for the DAG. Start schedules support cron expressions and one-off RFC 3339 timestamps with explicit offsets. Stop and restart remain cron-only.

skip_if_successful.description: When true, Dagu checks if this DAG has already succeeded since the last scheduled time. If it has, Dagu will skip the current scheduled run. This is useful for resource-intensive tasks or data processing jobs that shouldn't run twice. Note: Manual triggers always run regardless of this setting.

catchup_window.description: Lookback horizon for replaying missed cron runs when the scheduler restarts. Accepts Go duration syntax with day support (e.g. "6h", "2d12h", "30m"). Must be positive. If omitted, missed runs are not replayed.

overlap_policy.description: Controls what happens when a catchup run is ready but the DAG is still running from a previous run. "skip" (default) drops the catchup run and moves to the next. "all" keeps it in the buffer and retries on the next scheduler tick. "latest" discards all but the most recent missed interval.

oneOf.description: Labels as a string. Can be comma-separated ("label1, label2") or space-separated key=value format ("env=prod team=platform")
Labels as key-value pairs in map notation
Simple label or key=value format
Single key-value pair
Array of labels (strings or key-value objects)

labels.description: Labels for categorizing and filtering DAGs. Supports multiple formats: string ("label1, label2" or "env=prod team=platform"), map ({ env: prod }), or array (["label1", { env: prod }])

oneOf.description: Deprecated alias for labels. Can be comma-separated ("label1, label2") or space-separated key=value format ("env=prod team=platform")
Deprecated alias for labels as key-value pairs in map notation
Deprecated alias for a simple label or key=value format
Deprecated alias for a single key-value pair
Deprecated alias for an array of labels (strings or key-value objects)

tags.description: Deprecated alias for labels. Use labels for categorizing and filtering DAGs.

type.description: Execution type for steps. 'chain' (default) executes steps sequentially in the order they are defined, with each step automatically depending on the previous one. 'graph' uses dependency-based execution where steps run based on their 'depends' field. 'agent' is reserved for future agent-based execution.

oneOf.description: Map format: {KEY: value}
Key=Value format: 'KEY=value'
Array of environment variable definitions (map format or Key=Value strings)
Map format: {KEY1: value1, KEY2: value2}

env.description: Environment variables available to all steps in the DAG. Supports three formats: 1) Map format: env: {KEY: value}, 2) Array of maps: env: [{KEY: value}], 3) Array of Key=Value strings: env: ['KEY=value'] (Docker/docker-compose compatible). Can use shell expansions (${VAR}), references to other environment variables, or command substitutions (`cmd`). Note: These won't be stored in execution history data for security.

secrets.description: List of external secret references resolved at runtime and exposed as environment variables. Each entry declares the target environment variable name, provider, key, and optional provider-specific options.

continue_on.description: Default continue-on behavior for all steps

repeat_policy.description: Default repeat policy for all steps

timeout_sec.description: Default timeout in seconds for all steps

mail_on_error.description: Default mail-on-error flag for all steps

signal_on_stop.description: Default signal to send when stopping steps (e.g., SIGTERM)

env.description: Default environment variables for all steps (additive with step-level env)

preconditions.description: Default preconditions for all steps (additive with step-level preconditions)

agent.description: Default agent configuration for all agent-type steps. Each field is applied only when the step does not set its own value.

defaults.description: Default values for step configuration fields. Steps inherit these defaults and can override them individually. Most fields use full replacement when overridden; env and preconditions are additive.

log_dir.description: Base directory for storing logs. Defaults to ${HOME}/.local/share/logs if not specified.

dir.description: Base directory for storing artifacts for this DAG. Defaults to the global artifact_dir when omitted.

artifacts.description: Artifact storage configuration for this DAG.

log_output.description: Controls how stdout and stderr are logged. 'separate' writes stdout to .out and stderr to .err files. 'merged' writes both to a single .log file with interleaved output.

init.description: Step to execute before any workflow steps run (after preconditions pass). If this fails, the DAG fails and no steps execute.

abort.description: Step to execute when the DAG is aborted.

handler_on.description: Lifecycle event hooks that define commands to execute at various points in the DAG lifecycle: init (before steps), success, failure, abort, and exit (always runs last).

step_types.description: Reusable custom step type definitions for this DAG. Base config and DAG-local step_types are merged per document, and duplicate names across scopes are rejected.

oneOf.description: SMTP server port as string
SMTP server port as number

smtp.description: SMTP server configuration for sending email notifications.

failure.description: Send email notification when DAG fails

success.description: Send email notification when DAG succeeds

mail_on.description: Configuration for sending email notifications on DAG success or failure.

error_mail.description: Email configuration specifically for error notifications.

timeout_sec.description: Maximum number of seconds allowed for the entire DAG to finish. If exceeded, the DAG is considered timed out.

delay_sec.description: Delay in seconds before starting the first node. Useful for staggering workloads.

restart_wait_sec.description: Number of seconds to wait before restarting a failed or stopped DAG. Typically used with a process supervisor.

hist_retention_days.description: Number of days to retain execution history. After this period, older run logs/history can be purged.

max_active_runs.description: DEPRECATED: This field is ignored for local (DAG-based) queues. For concurrency control, define a global queue in config and use the 'queue' field.

queue.description: Name of the queue to assign this DAG to. If not specified, defaults to the DAG name. Used with global queue configuration to control concurrent execution across multiple DAGs.

max_active_steps.description: Maximum number of concurrent steps that can be active at once. Useful for limiting resource usage.

max_clean_up_time_sec.description: Maximum time in seconds to spend cleaning up (stopping steps, finalizing logs) before forcing shutdown. If exceeded, processes will be killed.

max_output_size.description: Maximum size in bytes for the output of each step. If a step's output exceeds this limit, it will fail with an error. Defaults to 1MB (1048576 bytes). This limit also applies to pattern matching in preconditions and continue_on conditions.

preconditions.description: Conditions that must be satisfied before the DAG can run. Can use shell expansions or command substitutions to validate external states.

oneOf.description: Space-separated positional parameters accessible as $1, $2, etc.

Ordered parameter entries. Supports legacy strings, legacy KEY: value pairs, and rich parameter definitions declared as objects with a required name field plus at least one additional definition field.

params.description: Default parameters that can be overridden when triggering the DAG. Supports five formats: 1) String format: space-separated positional parameters (e.g., 'arg1 arg2') accessible as $1, $2. 2) Array/Object format: named parameters as key-value pairs accessible as ${KEY}. 3) Inline rich definitions in array form, where each definition is an object with a required 'name' field plus optional fields such as type, default, enum, minimum, maximum, min_length, max_length, pattern, description, and required. 4) Top-level inline JSON Schema format using `type: object` plus `properties`. 5) Schema validation format: object with 'schema' (JSON Schema path/URL, inline object, or boolean schema) and optional 'values' (parameter values) for runtime validation and defaults.

container.description: Default container configuration for all steps in the DAG. Steps can override this configuration with their own container settings.

oneOf.description: Entire Docker authentication configuration as a JSON string, using the same format as DOCKER_AUTH_CONFIG or ~/.docker/config.json. Example: {"auths":{"docker.io":{"auth":"base64(username:password)"}}}
Authentication value as a string. Can be: 1) base64-encoded 'username:password', 2) JSON string with username/password fields, or 3) environment variable reference like ${DOCKER_AUTH}

username.description: Registry username. Supports environment variable expansion (e.g., ${DOCKER_USERNAME}).

password.description: Registry password or access token. Supports environment variable expansion (e.g., ${DOCKER_PASSWORD}).

auth.description: Pre-encoded authentication string. Should be base64(username:password) or a JSON string with credentials. Use this instead of username/password for pre-encoded credentials.

oneOf.description: Authentication configuration object with username/password or pre-encoded auth string.

Map of registry hostnames to authentication configurations. Registry hostnames should match the registry in your image names (e.g., 'docker.io', 'ghcr.io', 'gcr.io', '123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com').

registry_auths.description: Authentication configuration for Docker container registries. Allows pulling images from private registries. Can be specified as a complete DOCKER_AUTH_CONFIG JSON string, or as a map of individual registry credentials. Authentication priority: 1) DAG-level registry_auths, 2) DOCKER_AUTH_CONFIG environment variable, 3) No authentication (public registries). Supports environment variable expansion with ${VAR} syntax.

ssh.description: Default SSH configuration for all steps in the DAG. Steps can override this configuration with their own SSH settings. Note: SSH and container configurations are mutually exclusive at the DAG level.

s3.description: Default S3 configuration for all S3 steps in the DAG. Steps can override this configuration with their own S3 settings. Supports AWS S3 and S3-compatible services like MinIO.

llm.description: Default LLM configuration for all chat steps in the DAG. Steps with type: chat inherit this configuration if they don't specify their own llm field. If a step specifies llm, it completely overrides the DAG-level configuration.

harness.description: Default harness configuration for coding-agent steps in the DAG. Steps with type: harness inherit these defaults, and steps without an explicit type are treated as harness steps when this block is present. Step-level with overrides primary keys and replaces DAG-level fallback when fallback is set.

harnesses.description: Reusable custom harness definitions available to harness steps. Each definition describes how to invoke a named harness CLI; steps reference definitions via with.provider.

oneOf.description: List of steps that define the DAG. Steps can be: 1) Step objects with full configuration, 2) Strings for simple commands (shorthand syntax), or 3) Nested arrays containing parallel steps that run simultaneously.
Map of step names to step definitions. Steps can depend on each other, forming a directed acyclic graph.

otel.description: OpenTelemetry tracing configuration for distributed tracing of DAG execution.

disable_param_edit.description: When true, prevents users from modifying DAG parameters when starting the DAG. Defaults to false (users can edit parameters).

disable_run_id_edit.description: When true, prevents users from entering custom run IDs when starting the DAG. Defaults to false (users can enter custom run IDs).

run_config.description: Configuration for controlling user interactions when starting DAG runs. Allows restricting parameter editing and custom run ID input.

oneOf.description: Key-value pairs specifying worker label requirements. Values can be strings, numbers, or booleans (non-string values are coerced to strings). For example: {"gpu": true, "memory": "64G"} requires a worker with gpu=true and memory=64G labels.
Set to "local" to force this DAG to run locally even when the server default execution mode is distributed.

worker_selector.description: Worker selection for this DAG. Use a map of label key-value pairs to target specific workers, or the string "local" to force local execution. This setting applies to the entire DAG; individual steps can override this with their own worker_selector.

oneOf.description: Shell command with optional arguments (e.g., '/bin/bash -e', 'bash -eo pipefail')
Shell command as array (e.g., ['/bin/bash', '-e', '-o', 'pipefail'])

shell.description: Default shell to use for all steps in this DAG. Can be specified as a string with arguments (e.g., '/bin/bash -e') which will be automatically tokenized, or as an array for explicit argument separation. If not specified, the system default shell ($SHELL or /bin/sh) is used. Can be overridden at the step level.

redis.description: Default Redis configuration for all steps in the DAG. Steps with type: redis inherit this configuration if they don't specify their own connection settings.

kubernetes.description: Default Kubernetes configuration for explicit k8s / kubernetes steps in the DAG. The same fields as step-level Kubernetes config are supported, but image is optional at the DAG level.

limit.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for a failed step. Use 0 to disable automatic retries for that step.

interval_sec.description: Seconds to wait between retry attempts

oneOf.description: When true, uses default multiplier of 2.0
Custom exponential backoff multiplier

backoff.description: Exponential backoff multiplier for retry delays

max_interval_sec.description: Maximum interval in seconds (caps exponential growth)

exit_code.description: List of exit codes that should trigger a retry. If not specified, all non-zero exit codes will trigger a retry.

stepRetryPolicy.description: Configuration for automatically retrying failed steps.

limit.description: Maximum number of scheduler-issued DAG retry attempts. Use 0 to disable DAG-level automatic retries.

interval_sec.description: Seconds to wait between retry attempts

oneOf.description: When true, uses default multiplier of 2.0; false keeps a fixed interval
Custom exponential backoff multiplier

backoff.description: Exponential backoff multiplier for retry delays

max_interval_sec.description: Maximum interval in seconds (caps exponential growth)

dagRetryPolicy.description: Retry policy for automatic DAG-level retries after failure

oneOf.description: Path or URL to a JSON Schema document.
Inline JSON Schema object.
Boolean JSON Schema (true or false).

jsonSchemaDeclaration.description: JSON Schema declaration. Can be a path/URL string, an inline schema object, or a boolean schema.

dagParamLegacyEntry.description: Single legacy DAG parameter entry written as {KEY: value}.

dagParamRichEntry.description: Rich DAG parameter definition written as an object with a required name field and at least one additional definition field. Types drive validation and UI metadata; runtime env values remain strings. The optional eval field computes the effective default during normal DAG builds unless evaluation is disabled.

schema.description: JSON Schema declaration used for parameter validation.

values.description: Default parameter values to be validated against the schema.

dagParamExternalSchema.description: JSON Schema validation mode. This top-level params object is treated as schema-backed only when it is unambiguous: use `values` for explicit defaults, provide an inline object schema directly, or provide a path-like/URL schema reference by itself. Boolean schemas require `values` so legacy maps like `params: { schema: true }` remain valid.

dagParamLegacyMap.description: Legacy named DAG parameters as a map of key-value pairs, accessible as ${KEY}. Non-scalar YAML values are accepted and will be stringified by the loader.

dagParamInlineSchema.description: Top-level inline JSON Schema for DAG params. This form is recognized only when `type` is `object` and `properties` is present.

name.description: Environment variable name that will receive the secret value.

provider.description: Secret provider identifier (e.g., env, file, custom providers).

key.description: Provider-specific key or identifier used to look up the secret.

options.description: Provider-specific configuration options. Values must be strings to support serialization.

secretRef.description: Reference to an external secret that Dagu resolves at runtime. The resolved value is injected as an environment variable and masked in logs/output.

oneOf.description: Shorthand syntax: a simple command string that will be converted to a step with 'command' field
Shorthand syntax: a simple command string

Nested array representing parallel steps. All steps in this array will run simultaneously, automatically depending on the previous sequential step, and the next sequential step will depend on all of them.

name.description: Unique identifier for the step within this DAG. If omitted, Dagu will automatically generate a name.

id.description: Optional short identifier for the step. Can be used in variable references like ${id.stdout} to access step properties. Must be unique within the DAG if specified.

description.description: Brief description of what this step does. Helps document the step's purpose.

working_dir.description: Working directory for the step. Inherits from DAG's working_dir if not specified. Overrides DAG-level working_dir for this step.

oneOf.description: Single command to execute
Multiple commands to execute sequentially. Each string is a separate command.

command.description: Command(s) to execute. Can be a single shell command string, or an array of commands to run sequentially (stops on first failure).

exec.description: Structured direct-exec form for running a binary with explicit argv and no shell parsing. Mutually exclusive with command and script.

oneOf.description: Shell command with optional arguments (e.g., '/bin/bash -e', 'bash -eo pipefail')
Shell command as array (e.g., ['/bin/bash', '-e', '-o', 'pipefail'])

shell.description: Shell to use for executing the command. Can be specified as a string with arguments (e.g., '/bin/bash -e') which will be automatically tokenized, or as an array for explicit argument separation. Overrides DAG-level shell if specified. Defaults to DAG-level shell, or $SHELL/sh if not specified.

shell_packages.description: List of packages to install before executing the step. Useful for ensuring dependencies are available. It's only available when the shell is nix-shell.

script.description: Multi-line script content that will be executed. Gets piped into the command if specified, otherwise uses default shell.

stdout.description: File path where the step's standard output (stdout) will be written.

stderr.description: File path where the step's standard error (stderr) will be written.

log_output.description: Override DAG-level log output mode. 'separate' writes stdout to .out and stderr to .err files. 'merged' writes both to a single .log file.

oneOf.description: Variable name to capture the command's stdout.

name.description: Variable name to capture the command's stdout (required).

key.description: Custom key for this output in outputs.json. If not specified, the variable name is converted to camelCase.

omit.description: When true, excludes this output from outputs.json.

schema.description: JSON Schema declaration used to validate captured output. Output must be valid JSON matching this schema or the step fails.

output.description: Variable name to capture the command's stdout. Can be a string or an object with name, key, omit, and schema fields. This output can be referenced in subsequent steps.

oneOf.description: Name of a step that must complete successfully before this step can start.
List of step names that must complete successfully before this step can start.
Shorthand: 'skipped' to continue if step is skipped, 'failed' to continue if step fails

failure.description: Continue dag-run even if this step fails

skipped.description: Continue dag-run even if this step is skipped due to preconditions

oneOf.description: Exit code that should be treated as successful
Exit code represented as a string (parsed as integer at runtime)
List of exit codes that should be treated as successful
Output text or pattern that indicates success. Supports regex with 're:' prefix.

items.description: Output text or patterns that indicate success. Supports regex with 're:' prefix.

mark_success.description: Mark the step as successful even if it technically failed but met continue conditions

continue_on.description: Conditions under which the DAG should continue executing even if this step fails or is skipped. Can be a string ('skipped' or 'failed') or an object with detailed configuration.

repeat.description: Determines if and how the step should be repeated. Can be a boolean or a string ('while' or 'until').

limit.description: Maximum number of times this step will be executed. Once reached, the step stops repeating regardless of other conditions.

oneOf.description: When true, uses default multiplier of 2.0
Custom exponential backoff multiplier

backoff.description: Exponential backoff multiplier for repeat delays

max_interval_sec.description: Maximum interval in seconds (caps exponential growth)

condition.description: Command or expression to evaluate for repeat-until. Can include shell commands, environment variables, or command substitutions.

expected.description: Expected value or pattern to match against the condition result. Supports regex patterns with 're:' prefix.

exit_code.description: Exit code or list of exit codes that will stop repetition. If not specified, all non-zero exit codes will trigger repetition.

repeat_policy.description: Configuration for repeatedly executing this step at fixed intervals or until a condition is met. Supports string matching, command substitution, and exit code checks.

mail_on_error.description: Send an email notification if this specific step fails.

preconditions.description: Conditions that must be met before this step can run. Supports command exit codes, environment variables, and regex matching.

signal_on_stop.description: Signal to send when stopping this step (e.g., SIGINT). If empty, uses same signal as parent process.

timeout_sec.description: Maximum execution time for the step in seconds. If set, this timeout takes precedence over the DAG-level timeout for this step.

call.description: Name of a DAG to execute as a sub dag-run.

oneOf.description: Space-separated positional parameters accessible as $1, $2, etc.
Named parameters as key-value pairs
Named parameters as key-value pairs

params.description: Parameters for the step. Supports three formats: 1) String format: space-separated positional parameters (e.g., 'arg1 arg2') accessible as $1, $2. 2) Array format: named parameters as key-value objects preserving rich types (numbers, booleans, nested objects). 3) Object format: named parameters as simple key-value pairs. For sub-DAG execution (when 'call' or 'run' is specified), these are passed to the sub DAG and accessible as ${KEY}. Rich types are preserved when passing to executors that support them.

oneOf.description: Variable containing array of items to process in parallel
Array of items to process in parallel
Variable containing array of items
Array of items to process in parallel

max_concurrent.description: Maximum number of concurrent executions (default: 10, maximum: 1000)

oneOf.description: Parallel execution configuration with concurrency control

parallel.description: Configuration for parallel execution of sub DAGs. Only applicable when 'run' is specified. Allows processing multiple items concurrently using the same workflow definition.

oneOf.description: Key-value pairs specifying worker label requirements. Values can be strings, numbers, or booleans (non-string values are coerced to strings). For example: {"gpu": true, "memory": "64G"} requires a worker with gpu=true and memory=64G labels.
Set to "local" to force this step to run locally even when the server default execution mode is distributed.

worker_selector.description: Worker selection for this step. Use a map of label key-value pairs to target specific workers, or the string "local" to force local execution.

oneOf.description: Map format: {KEY: value}
Key=Value format: 'KEY=value'
Array of environment variable definitions (map format or Key=Value strings)
Map format: {KEY1: value1, KEY2: value2}

env.description: Environment variables specific to this step. These override DAG-level environment variables with the same name. Supports three formats: 1) Map format: env: {KEY: value}, 2) Array of maps: env: [{KEY: value}], 3) Array of Key=Value strings: env: ['KEY=value'] (Docker/docker-compose compatible). Can use shell expansions (${VAR}), references to other environment variables, or command substitutions (`cmd`). When used with 'container' field, these are merged with container.env.

container.description: Container configuration for this step. When specified, the step runs in its own container instead of the DAG-level container. Uses the same configuration format as the DAG-level container field. Note: Cannot be used together with 'executor'.

type.description: Builtin executor type or a custom step type name declared in step_types or base config. If omitted, Dagu infers the builtin type from other step fields. Cannot be used together with 'executor' field.

with.description: Executor-specific configuration. For builtin types, this is the executor config object. For custom step types, this is the validated input object defined by step_types.<name>.input_schema.

config.description: Legacy alias for 'with'. Use 'with' for new DAGs.

llm.description: LLM configuration for AI-powered steps. Required when type is 'chat'. Not used by 'agent' type (agent resolves models from Agent Settings).

agent.description: Agent configuration for AI agent steps. Only valid when type is 'agent'. Controls model, tools, safety, and iteration limits.

prompt.description: Message displayed to the approver explaining what approval is needed.

input.description: List of expected input field names from the approver.

required.description: Subset of input fields that must be provided by the approver.

rewind_to.description: Optional step name or ID to restart from when the step is pushed back. Must reference the step itself or an upstream dependency.

approval.description: Human approval gate for this step. When configured, the step executes its command first, then pauses the DAG and waits for human approval before proceeding to dependent steps. The approver can provide input values that become available as environment variables in subsequent steps.

messages.description: Session messages for chat and agent steps. Only valid when type is 'chat' or 'agent'. Supports variable substitution with ${VAR}.

tools.description: List of tool DAG names available to the LLM for function calling. Only valid when type is 'chat'. Tools are other DAGs that can be called by the LLM to perform specific tasks.

value.description: Expression to evaluate for router step routing (e.g., '${STATUS}'). Only used when type is 'router'. Router steps require DAG type 'graph'.

routes.description: Map of patterns to target step names for routing. Keys are patterns (exact match or 're:regex'), values are arrays of step names. Target steps automatically depend on the router (no explicit depends needed). Each step can only be targeted by one route. Only used when type is 'router'. Router steps require DAG type 'graph'.

condition.description: Command or expression to evaluate. Can include shell commands, environment variables, or command substitutions with backticks.

command.description: Deprecated alias for condition. Still accepted for backward compatibility.

expected.description: Expected value or pattern to match against the condition result. Supports regex patterns with 're:' prefix (e.g., 're:0[1-9]' for matching numbers 01-09).

negate.description: If true, inverts the condition result. The DAG/step will run when the condition does NOT match the expected value.

condition.description: Defines a condition that must be met before execution. Used in preconditions at both DAG and step levels.

from.description: Email address to use as the sender address for notifications.

oneOf.description: Single email address to receive notifications.
List of email addresses to receive notifications.

to.description: Email address(es) to receive notifications. Can be a single string or an array of strings.

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the email subject line. Useful for filtering or categorizing notification emails.

attach_logs.description: When true, relevant log files will be attached to the notification email.

mailConfig.description: Configuration for email notifications, used by error_mail and info_mail settings.

enabled.description: Enable or disable OpenTelemetry tracing for this DAG. When false, no traces will be exported.

endpoint.description: OTLP endpoint URL for exporting traces. Supports both gRPC (e.g., http://localhost:4317) and HTTP (e.g., http://localhost:4318/v1/traces) protocols.

headers.description: Custom headers to include with OTLP export requests. Useful for authentication (e.g., Authorization: Bearer token).

insecure.description: Skip TLS certificate verification when connecting to the OTLP endpoint. Only use in development environments.

timeout.description: Timeout for exporting traces to the OTLP endpoint. Accepts duration strings like '30s', '1m', '5m'.

service.name.description: Service name for the traces. Defaults to 'dagu' if not specified. Can use variables like ${DAG_NAME}.

service.version.description: Version of the service producing the traces.

resource.description: Resource attributes to attach to all spans. Common attributes include service.name, service.version, and deployment.environment.

otelConfig.description: OpenTelemetry configuration for distributed tracing. Enables detailed execution traces for DAGs and steps, providing visibility into workflow performance and debugging.

oneOf.description: Name of an existing running container to exec into. Dagu waits up to 120 seconds for the container to be in running state; fails if not running within timeout.

container.description: Container configuration. Can be either a string (name of existing container to exec into) or an object (container configuration with image or exec field).

exec.description: Name of an existing running container to exec into. Dagu waits up to 120 seconds for the container to be running; fails if not running within timeout. Mutually exclusive with 'image'. When used, only 'user', 'working_dir', and 'env' are applicable.

name.description: Custom container name. If empty, Docker generates a random name. Must be unique - if a container with this name already exists (running or stopped), the DAG will fail. Not applicable in exec mode.

image.description: Container image to use (e.g., 'python:3.11', 'node:20'). Mutually exclusive with 'exec'.

oneOf.description: Boolean shorthand: true = 'always', false = 'never'
String policy: 'always' pulls the image every time, 'never' uses only local images, 'missing' pulls only if not present locally

pull_policy.description: Image pull policy. Accepts boolean (true='always', false='never') or string ('always', 'never', 'missing'). Default is 'missing'. Not applicable in exec mode.

env.description: Environment variables to set in the container. Supports maps or arrays combining maps and KEY=value strings. Values can include variable expansion (e.g., 'API_KEY=${API_KEY}').

volumes.description: Volume mounts for the container. Format: 'host_path:container_path[:mode]' where mode is 'ro' for read-only or 'rw' (default) for read-write. Relative paths (starting with './' or '.') are resolved relative to the DAG's working_dir. Not applicable in exec mode.

user.description: User (and optionally group) to run the container as. Format: 'user' or 'user:group' (e.g., '1000' or '1000:1000').

working_dir.description: Working directory inside the container. Commands will be executed from this directory.

platform.description: Platform for the container (e.g., 'linux/amd64', 'linux/arm64'). Useful for ensuring compatibility on different architectures. Not applicable in exec mode.

ports.description: Ports to expose from the container. Format: 'host_port:container_port' or just 'container_port'. Not applicable in exec mode.

network.description: Network mode for the container (e.g., 'host', 'bridge', 'none', or a custom network name). Not applicable in exec mode.

startup.description: Startup mode for the DAG-level container: 'keepalive' uses an internal keepalive/sleep, 'entrypoint' honors the image's ENTRYPOINT/CMD, 'command' runs the provided command array. Not applicable in exec mode.

command.description: Command to execute when startup is 'command'. Must be a non-empty array. Not applicable in exec mode.

wait_for.description: Readiness condition before steps execute: 'running' waits for container to run; 'healthy' waits for Docker healthcheck to report healthy. Not applicable in exec mode.

log_pattern.description: Optional regex; if set, waits for this pattern to appear in container logs before steps run. Not applicable in exec mode.

restart_policy.description: Docker restart policy for the container: 'no', 'always', or 'unless-stopped'. Not applicable in exec mode.

keep_container.description: Keep the container running after the step completes. Useful for debugging or when running services that need to persist between steps. Not applicable in exec mode.

test.description: Command to run to check health. Must start with 'NONE' (disable healthcheck), 'CMD' (run command directly), or 'CMD-SHELL' (run command in shell). Examples: ['CMD', 'pg_isready', '-U', 'postgres'] or ['CMD-SHELL', 'curl -f http://localhost/ || exit 1'].

interval.description: Time between health checks (e.g., '5s', '1m'). Default is 30s.

timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for a health check to complete (e.g., '3s', '10s'). Default is 30s.

start_period.description: Grace period for the container to initialize before health checks start counting failures (e.g., '10s', '1m'). Default is 0s.

retries.description: Number of consecutive failures needed to mark the container as unhealthy. Default is 3.

healthcheck.description: Custom healthcheck configuration for the container. Allows 'wait_for: healthy' to work with images that don't have built-in healthchecks (e.g., postgres, mysql). Not applicable in exec mode.

shell.description: Shell wrapper for executing step commands. Format: first element is the shell executable, remaining elements are flags, and the step command is appended as the final argument. Example: ["/bin/bash", "-o", "errexit", "-c"]. The '-c' flag (or equivalent) should be the last flag. Works in both exec and image modes.

containerConfig.description: Container configuration object. Either 'exec' (to run in an existing container) or 'image' (to create a new container) must be specified, but not both.

host.description: Hostname or IP address of the SSH server.

key.description: Path to the SSH private key file. If not specified, defaults are tried: ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ecdsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/id_dsa.

password.description: SSH password for authentication (not recommended; prefer key-based auth).

strict_host_key.description: Enable strict host key checking. When true (default), verifies the server's host key against known_hosts. Set to false only for testing.

known_host_file.description: Path to the known_hosts file for host key verification. Defaults to ~/.ssh/known_hosts.

oneOf.description: Shell command with optional arguments (e.g., '/bin/bash -e').
Shell command expressed as an array for explicit argument separation (e.g., ['/bin/bash', '-e', '-o', 'pipefail']).

shell.description: Shell for remote command execution. Commands are wrapped as: shell -c 'command'. Providing arguments (via string or array form) enables shell features like variable expansion, pipes, and command chaining on the remote server.

sshConfig.description: SSH configuration for remote command execution. Can be specified at DAG level for reuse across steps, or at step level for specific overrides. Password authentication is supported but key-based auth is recommended.

region.description: AWS region (e.g., 'us-east-1', 'eu-west-1'). If not specified, uses AWS SDK default resolution.

endpoint.description: Custom S3-compatible endpoint URL. Use for MinIO, LocalStack, or other S3-compatible services (e.g., 'http://localhost:9000').

access_key_id.description: AWS access key ID. Supports environment variable expansion (e.g., '${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID}').

secret_access_key.description: AWS secret access key. Supports environment variable expansion (e.g., '${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}').

session_token.description: AWS session token for temporary credentials. Supports environment variable expansion.

bucket.description: Default S3 bucket name. Can be overridden at step level.

force_path_style.description: Use path-style addressing (bucket in path instead of subdomain). Required for most S3-compatible services like MinIO.

disable_ssl.description: Disable SSL/TLS for connections. Only use for local development with non-TLS endpoints.

s3ConnectionConfig.description: Common S3 connection configuration shared between DAG-level and step-level S3 settings.

key.description: S3 object key (path). Required for upload, download, and single-object delete operations.

source.description: Local file path for upload operations. Required for 'upload' command.

destination.description: Local file path for download operations. Required for 'download' command.

prefix.description: S3 key prefix for list and batch delete operations.

content_type.description: Content-Type header for uploaded objects. Auto-detected from file extension if not specified.

storage_class.description: S3 storage class for uploaded objects.

metadata.description: Custom metadata key-value pairs for uploaded objects.

tags.description: S3 object tags as key-value pairs.

sse_kms_key_id.description: KMS key ID for SSE-KMS encryption.

part_size.description: Part size in MB for multipart uploads. Minimum 5MB. Default: 10MB.

concurrency.description: Number of concurrent upload/download parts. Default: 5.

max_keys.description: Maximum keys to return per list request. Default: 1000.

delimiter.description: Delimiter for grouping keys in list operations (e.g., '/').

recursive.description: Recursively list all objects under prefix. Default: false.

output_format.description: Output format for list results. Default: json.

quiet.description: Suppress output for delete operations. Default: false.

s3ExecutorConfig.description: S3 executor configuration. Command specifies the operation: upload, download, list, or delete.

timeout.description: Request timeout in seconds. If exceeded, the request is cancelled.

headers.description: HTTP headers to include in the request. Key-value pairs where values are strings.

query.description: Query parameters to append to the URL. Key-value pairs where values are strings.

body.description: Request body content. Can be JSON, XML, or plain text depending on Content-Type header.

silent.description: When true, suppresses response headers and status in output on success.

debug.description: Enable debug mode to log detailed request/response information.

json.description: When true, formats output as JSON including status_code, headers, and parsed body.

format.description: Response output format. Use "json" for structured output.

skip_tls_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification. WARNING: Only use for testing with self-signed certificates.

httpExecutorConfig.description: Configuration options for HTTP executor requests.

host.description: Hostname or IP address of the SSH server. Required.

ip.description: Alias for 'host'. IP address of the SSH server.

key.description: Path to SSH private key file. If not specified, defaults are tried: ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ecdsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/id_dsa.

password.description: SSH password for authentication. Not recommended; prefer key-based authentication.

strict_host_key.description: Enable strict host key checking. When true (default), verifies server's host key against known_hosts.

known_host_file.description: Path to known_hosts file for host key verification.

oneOf.description: Shell command with optional arguments (e.g., '/bin/bash -e').
Shell command expressed as an array for explicit argument separation (e.g., ['/bin/bash', '-e', '-o', 'pipefail']).

shell.description: Shell for remote command execution. Commands are wrapped as: shell -c 'command'. Providing arguments (via string or array form) enables shell features like variable expansion, pipes, and command chaining on the remote server.

sshExecutorConfig.description: SSH executor configuration for remote command execution.

host.description: Hostname or IP address of the SSH server. Required.

ip.description: Alias for 'host'. IP address of the SSH server.

key.description: Path to SSH private key file. If not specified, defaults are tried: ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ecdsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/id_dsa.

password.description: SSH password for authentication. Not recommended; prefer key-based authentication.

strict_host_key.description: Enable strict host key checking. When true (default), verifies server's host key against known_hosts.

known_host_file.description: Path to known_hosts file for host key verification.

source.description: Source path. For upload, this is a local path; for download, this is a remote path.

destination.description: Destination path. For upload, this is a remote path; for download, this is a local path.

key.description: Path to bastion private key file.

sftpExecutorConfig.description: SFTP executor configuration for file transfers over SSH.

image.description: Docker image to use for creating a new container (e.g., 'python:3.11', 'alpine:latest').

container_name.description: Name or ID of an existing container to exec into. Use this instead of image to run commands in an existing container.

oneOf.description: Boolean shorthand: true = 'always', false = 'never'
Pull policy string

pull.description: Image pull policy. 'always' pulls every time, 'never' uses only local, 'missing' pulls if not present.

auto_remove.description: Automatically remove the container after it exits.

working_dir.description: Working directory inside the container. Shortcut that maps to container.WorkingDir.

volumes.description: Volume mounts. Format: 'host_path:container_path[:mode]'. Shortcut that maps to host.Binds.

container.description: Docker container configuration (docker/api/types/container.Config). See Docker API documentation.

host.description: Docker host configuration (docker/api/types/container.HostConfig). See Docker API documentation.

network.description: Docker network configuration (docker/api/types/network.NetworkingConfig). See Docker API documentation.

exec.description: Docker exec options (docker/api/types/container.ExecOptions). Only valid with container_name.

dockerExecutorConfig.description: Docker executor configuration for running commands in containers.

oneOf.description: Single recipient email address
List of recipient email addresses

attachments.description: List of file paths to attach to the email.

mailExecutorConfig.description: Configuration for sending emails via the mail executor.

raw.description: When true, outputs values without JSON formatting. Strings are printed without quotes, nulls print empty lines.

input.description: File path to read JSON input from. Mutually exclusive with script.

jqExecutorConfig.description: Configuration for JQ JSON query executor.

source.description: Source path for archive operations. Required for all operations.

destination.description: Destination path for archive operations. Required for 'create' operation (unless dry_run).

format.description: Archive format (auto-detected from extension if not specified). Supports: tar, tar.gz, tar.bz2, zip, etc.

compression_level.description: Compression level. -1 for default, 0 for no compression, 1-9 for gzip/bzip2 levels.

password.description: Password for encrypted archives. Only supported for extract/list operations.

strip_components.description: Number of leading path components to strip during extraction.

include.description: Glob patterns for files to include. If specified, only matching files are processed.

exclude.description: Glob patterns for files to exclude.

dry_run.description: Simulate the operation without making changes.

verbose.description: Enable verbose output showing processed files.

follow_symlinks.description: Follow symbolic links when creating archives.

continue_on_error.description: Continue processing even if individual files fail.

archiveExecutorConfig.description: Configuration for archive operations (create, extract, list).

input_file.description: Path to input file (CSV, TSV, or JSONL). Required.

format.description: Input format. Auto-detected from file extension if not specified.

has_header.description: Whether first row is a header row. Default: true for CSV/TSV files.

delimiter.description: Field delimiter. Default: ',' for csv, '\t' for tsv.

columns.description: Explicit column names. Overrides header row if specified.

null_values.description: Values to treat as NULL. Default: ['', 'NULL', 'null', '\N'].

batch_size.description: Number of rows per INSERT statement. Default: 1000.

on_conflict.description: Conflict handling strategy. Default: 'error'. For PostgreSQL, 'replace' requires 'conflict_target' to perform UPSERT (ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE); without it, falls back to DO NOTHING. SQLite uses INSERT OR REPLACE which replaces the entire row.

sqlImportConfig.description: Configuration for importing data from CSV/TSV/JSONL files into a database table.

max_open_conns.description: Maximum number of open connections. Default: 5.

max_idle_conns.description: Maximum number of idle connections. Default: 2.

conn_max_lifetime.description: Maximum connection lifetime in seconds. 0 = no limit. Default: 300.

oneOf.description: Named parameters as map (e.g., {name: 'value'}). Use :name in query.
Positional parameters as array. Use $1, $2, etc. in query.

params.description: Query parameters for SQL injection prevention.

advisory_lock.description: Named advisory lock. Prevents concurrent execution across distributed workers.

output_format.description: Output format for query results. Default: jsonl.

null_string.description: String representation for NULL values. Default: 'null'.

streaming.description: Stream results to file instead of stdout.

import.description: Import data from CSV/TSV/JSONL file into a table.

postgresExecutorConfig.description: PostgreSQL executor configuration for database operations.

dsn.description: SQLite connection string. Format: file:./path/to/db.sqlite or :memory: for in-memory database.

max_open_conns.description: Maximum number of open connections. Default: 5. Recommended: 1 for SQLite.

max_idle_conns.description: Maximum number of idle connections. Default: 2.

oneOf.description: Named parameters as map (e.g., {name: 'value'}). Use :name in query.
Positional parameters as array. Use ? placeholders in query.

params.description: Query parameters for SQL injection prevention.

file_lock.description: Use file locking for exclusive database access. Creates a .lock file.

output_format.description: Output format for query results. Default: jsonl.

null_string.description: String representation for NULL values. Default: 'null'.

streaming.description: Stream results to file instead of stdout.

import.description: Import data from CSV/TSV/JSONL file into a table.

sqliteExecutorConfig.description: SQLite executor configuration for database operations. Uses pure Go SQLite with automatic pragmas: foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout=5000.

url.description: Redis connection URL (redis://[user:pass@]host:port[/db]). Alternative to host/port.

host.description: Redis server hostname. Required if URL not provided.

tls_key.description: Client key path for mTLS. Must be provided with tls_cert.

tls_ca.description: CA certificate path for TLS verification.

tls_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification. WARNING: Only use for testing.

sentinel_master.description: Sentinel master name. Required when mode is 'sentinel'.

sentinel_addrs.description: Sentinel server addresses. Required when mode is 'sentinel'.

cluster_addrs.description: Cluster node addresses. Required when mode is 'cluster'.

redisConnectionConfig.description: Common Redis connection configuration shared between DAG-level and step-level Redis settings.

command.description: Redis command to execute (e.g., 'GET', 'SET', 'HSET'). Required unless using script/pipeline.

keys.description: Multiple keys for commands like MGET, DEL.

value.description: Value for SET, LPUSH, etc. Can be string, number, boolean, or object.

values.description: Multiple values for commands like MSET, LPUSH.

fields.description: Multiple hash fields for HMSET, HGETALL.

ttl.description: Expiration time in seconds for SET with EX option.

nx.description: SET only if key does not exist.

keep_ttl.description: Preserve existing TTL when updating key (Redis 6+).

count.description: Count parameter for SCAN, LPOP, RPOP.

start.description: Range start index for LRANGE, ZRANGE.

stop.description: Range stop index for LRANGE, ZRANGE.

message.description: Message to publish. Can be string, number, boolean, or object.

stream_id.description: Stream message ID. Use '*' for auto-generation.

block.description: Block timeout in milliseconds for XREAD, XREADGROUP.

script_sha.description: SHA of pre-loaded script. Use EVALSHA instead of EVAL.

pipeline.description: Batch of commands to execute in a pipeline.

watch.description: Keys to WATCH for optimistic locking.

lock.description: Distributed lock name. Acquires lock before executing command.

lock_wait.description: Wait between lock retries in milliseconds. Default: 100.

null_value.description: String representation for nil values. Default: 'null'.

max_result_size.description: Maximum result size in bytes. Default: 10MB.

redisExecutorConfig.description: Redis executor configuration for executing Redis commands, managing cache, pub/sub, streams, and distributed locks.

harnessConfigValue.description: Harness flag value. Scalars become a single CLI flag value; arrays become repeated flags.

binary.description: CLI binary name or path for the custom harness.

prefix_args.description: Arguments that always appear before prompt placement and runtime flags.

prompt_mode.description: How the prompt is passed to the CLI.

prompt_flag.description: Flag token used when prompt_mode is flag.

prompt_position.description: Whether prompt tokens appear before or after runtime flags.

flag_style.description: Default flag prefix style for runtime options that do not have an explicit option_flags override.

option_flags.description: Per-option override mapping from config key to exact CLI flag token.

harnessDefinitions.description: Reusable named custom harness definitions. Null entries remove inherited definitions from base config.

provider.description: Harness provider name. May reference a built-in provider such as claude, codex, copilot, opencode, or pi, or a custom top-level harnesses entry. Supports ${VAR} interpolation.

harnessProviderConfig.description: Primary or fallback harness provider configuration. Reserved keys are provider and fallback; all other keys are passed through to the CLI as flags.

provider.description: Harness provider name. May reference a built-in provider such as claude, codex, copilot, opencode, or pi, or a custom top-level harnesses entry. Supports ${VAR} interpolation.

fallback.description: Ordered fallback harness provider configs tried after the primary config fails. The fallback key is reserved and is not passed through as a CLI flag.

harnessExecutorConfig.description: Harness step configuration. Unknown keys are passed through to the CLI as flags. When no DAG-level harness defaults exist, specify provider.

harnessDefaultsConfig.description: DAG-level harness defaults. Specify provider plus optional CLI flags and fallback providers.

name.description: Name of the Secret or ConfigMap.

key.description: Key to read from the referenced Secret or ConfigMap.

kubernetesKeySelector.description: Reference to a specific key in a Secret or ConfigMap.

field_path.description: Pod field path to expose as an environment variable value (for example, metadata.name).

kubernetesFieldRef.description: Reference to a Pod field for downward API environment variables.

secret_key_ref.description: Read the value from a specific key in a Secret.

config_map_key_ref.description: Read the value from a specific key in a ConfigMap.

field_ref.description: Read the value from a Pod field.

kubernetesEnvVarSource.description: Source configuration for an environment variable value. Exactly one source must be specified.

value_from.description: Dynamic source for the environment variable value.

kubernetesEnvVar.description: A Kubernetes environment variable entry. Set either value or value_from, but not both.

name.description: Name of the Secret or ConfigMap to import.

kubernetesEnvFromRef.description: Reference to a Secret or ConfigMap for env_from.

config_map_ref.description: Import all keys from a ConfigMap.

prefix.description: Optional prefix applied to imported environment variable names.

secret_ref.description: Import all keys from a Secret.

prefix.description: Optional prefix applied to imported environment variable names.

kubernetesEnvFromSource.description: Import a Secret or ConfigMap as environment variables. Exactly one source must be specified.

requests.description: Resource requests such as cpu and memory. Values use Kubernetes quantity syntax (for example, 100m, 128Mi).

limits.description: Resource limits such as cpu and memory. Values use Kubernetes quantity syntax (for example, 500m, 1Gi).

kubernetesResourceRequirements.description: CPU and memory requests and limits for the Job container.

localhost_profile.description: Localhost seccomp profile path. Required only when type is Localhost.

run_as_non_root.description: Require the container to run as non-root.

privileged.description: Run the container in privileged mode.

read_only_root_filesystem.description: Mount the container root filesystem read-only.

allow_privilege_escalation.description: Allow the process to gain more privileges.

run_as_non_root.description: Require containers to run as non-root by default.

kubernetesNodeSelector.description: Node selector. Empty object is allowed to clear inherited defaults.

topology_key.description: Topology key used for pod affinity.

status.description: Pod condition status. Defaults to True when omitted.

kubernetesPodFailurePolicyRule.description: A single pod failure policy rule.

rules.description: Pod failure policy rules. Empty array is allowed to clear inherited defaults.

medium.description: Storage medium for emptyDir, such as Memory.

size_limit.description: Optional size limit using Kubernetes quantity syntax.

kubernetesVolume.description: Volume definition. Exactly one volume source must be specified.

kubernetesVolumeMount.description: Volume mount for the Job container.

image.description: Container image to run. Optional in raw step YAML because DAG or base kubernetes defaults may provide it, but required in the effective step config used at runtime.

namespace.description: Kubernetes namespace for the Job. Default: default.

kubeconfig.description: Explicit path to a kubeconfig file.

service_account.description: Service account name for the Pod.

image_pull_policy.description: Image pull policy. Accepted values are Always, IfNotPresent, or Never (case-insensitive).

image_pull_secrets.description: Names of image pull secrets for private registries.

env.description: Environment variables for the Job container.

env_from.description: Bulk environment variable imports from Secrets or ConfigMaps.

resources.description: CPU and memory requests and limits.

node_selector.description: Node selector labels for Pod scheduling.

termination_grace_period_seconds.description: Grace period in seconds before the Pod is forcefully terminated.

labels.description: Labels applied to both the Job and Pod.

annotations.description: Annotations applied to both the Job and Pod.

volume_mounts.description: Volume mounts for the Job container.

active_deadline.description: Kubernetes Job active deadline in seconds.

cleanup_policy.description: Cleanup policy after execution. Accepted values are delete or keep (case-insensitive).

kubernetesCommonConfig.description: Common Kubernetes configuration shared by DAG-level defaults and step-level Kubernetes executor config.

kubernetesDefaultsConfig.description: Default Kubernetes configuration for explicit Kubernetes steps. Same fields as step-level with, but image is optional.

kubernetesExecutorConfig.description: Kubernetes executor configuration for running a workflow step as a Kubernetes Job.

provider.description: LLM provider to use (for single model config). Aliases 'ollama', 'vllm', 'llama' map to 'local'. Not required when using model array.

model.description: Model name (string) or array of model objects for fallback.

system.description: Default system prompt for sessions. Prepended to messages if specified.

temperature.description: Controls randomness in responses (0.0-2.0). Lower values are more deterministic.

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate in the response.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling parameter (0.0-1.0). Alternative to temperature for controlling randomness.

base_url.description: Custom API endpoint URL. Overrides the default endpoint for the provider.

api_key_name.description: Name of the environment variable containing the API key. If not specified, the default for the provider is used (e.g., OPENAI_API_KEY).

stream.description: Enable streaming output. When true (default), response tokens are streamed to stdout as they arrive.

thinking.description: Extended thinking/reasoning configuration. Enables deeper reasoning for complex tasks.

web_search.description: Provider-native web search configuration. Supported by Anthropic, Gemini, and OpenRouter.

llmConfig.description: LLM configuration. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, OpenRouter, and local models (Ollama/vLLM).

enabled.description: Enable provider-native web search. When true, the LLM can search the web for real-time information.

max_uses.description: Maximum number of search invocations per request.

allowed_domains.description: Restrict search results to these domains (Anthropic only).

blocked_domains.description: Exclude search results from these domains (Anthropic only).

user_location.description: Approximate location for search result localization.

webSearchConfig.description: Configuration for provider-native web search. Anthropic uses web_search_20250305 tool, Gemini uses google_search grounding, OpenRouter uses web plugin.

enabled.description: Enable extended thinking/reasoning mode. When true, the model uses additional computation for more thorough reasoning.

effort.description: Reasoning effort level. Maps to provider-specific settings: low (minimal reasoning), medium (default), high (thorough), xhigh (maximum, OpenAI GPT-5.2+ only).

budget_tokens.description: Explicit token budget for thinking (provider-specific). Anthropic: 1024-128K, Gemini 2.5: 128-32768. Overrides effort-based defaults.

include_in_output.description: Include thinking blocks in stdout output. When false (default), only the final response is shown.

thinkingConfig.description: Configuration for extended thinking/reasoning capabilities. Provider support varies: Anthropic (Claude 3.7+), OpenAI (o1/o3/GPT-5), Gemini (2.5+), OpenRouter (unified).

role.description: Message role. 'system' for instructions, 'user' for prompts, 'assistant' for model responses.

content.description: Message content. Supports variable substitution with ${VAR} syntax.

llmMessage.description: A single message in an LLM session.

model.description: Override the global default model for this step. Must match a model ID configured in Agent Settings.

enabled.description: Whether to load global and per-DAG memory into agent context.

memory.description: Memory configuration for the agent step.

prompt.description: Additional instructions appended to the built-in system prompt.

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of tool call rounds before stopping. Default: 50.

safe_mode.description: Enable command approval via human review for bash commands. Default: true.

skills.description: List of skill IDs the agent is allowed to use. If omitted, falls back to globally enabled skills.

soul.description: Soul ID for this step's agent identity. When omitted, inherits from defaults.agent.soul or uses the built-in default.

web_search.description: Provider-native web search configuration for this agent step. Overrides the global agent web search setting.

agentStepConfig.description: Configuration for agent-type steps. Controls model selection, available tools, safety policies, and iteration limits.

enabled.description: List of tools to enable. When omitted, all tools are available.

agentToolsConfig.description: Configuration for which tools are available to the agent.

default_behavior.description: Default action when no rule matches.

deny_behavior.description: What happens when a command is denied: 'block' rejects it, 'ask_user' asks for approval.

rules.description: Ordered list of pattern-matching rules evaluated top-to-bottom.

agentBashPolicy.description: Security policy for bash commands executed by the agent.

pattern.description: Regex pattern to match against commands.

action.description: Action to take when the pattern matches.

agentBashRule.description: A single bash command policy rule.

builtinExecutorType.description: Type of executor to use for this step, including aliases such as k8s / kubernetes.

anyOf.description: Custom step type name declared in step_types or base config.

executorType.description: Builtin executor type or a custom step type name declared in step_types.

customStepTypes.description: Custom step type definitions keyed by user-defined type name.

type.description: Builtin executor type or builtin alias that this custom step expands to. Custom step types cannot target other custom step types.

description.description: Optional description applied when the expanded step does not set its own description.

input_schema.description: Inline JSON Schema object used to validate and default the custom step's with input.

template.description: Step fragment template expanded at build time. Use {$input: path.to.value} for typed injection and Go template strings for string interpolation.

customStepTypeDefinition.description: Definition of a reusable custom step type.

command.description: Executable path or binary name to run directly.

args.description: Explicit argv entries passed to the executable without shell parsing.

stepExec.description: Structured direct-exec form for command steps. Runs an executable with explicit argv and no shell parsing.

executorConfig.description: Executor-specific configuration. Schema depends on executor type.

config.description: Command/shell executors do not use executor.config. Configure at step level.
DAG/subworkflow executors do not use executor.config. Use step.call or step.run field.
Parallel executor does not use executor.config. Use step.parallel field.

executorObject.description: Executor configuration object with type-specific config validation.
